DISCLAIMER: I AM NOT AN ATTORNEY AND THIS IS ONLY THE DOCUMENT THAT WE USED. YOU SHOULD HAVE AN ATTORNEY REVIEW BEFORE USING THIS DOCUMENT. DISCLAIMER - MEGALODON MARKETING, LLC IS NOT RESPONSIBLE FOR ANY LIABILITY OF THE USE OF THIS DOCUMENT.





SEPARATION AGREEMENT



CONFIDENTIAL



[DATE]



[EMPLOYEE]



	Re: Agreement Governing Separation of Employment with [YOURCOMPANY], LLC.



Dear [EMPLOYEE]:



As we have discussed, this letter is confirmation that you (“You” or “Your”) have involuntarily resigned your employment position with [YOURCOMPANY], LLC  (the “Company”) effective [DATE] (the “Separation Date”).  This letter agreement (the “Agreement”) sets forth the terms under which Your relationship with the Company is ending.  Specifically, You and the Company (collectively, the “Parties”) agree as follows:



Separation Terms



1.	Obligations upon Separation:  Provided that You satisfy the conditions of this Agreement and do not revoke this Agreement, the Company will:



The Company will pay you an exit payment in an amount equal to two weeks wages, which shall be available to Employee within five (5) days after expiration of the seven (7) day revocation period as described in Section 4 below.  This payment shall be considered taxable compensation and the Company will withhold ordinary payroll taxes as required by state, local and federal law; and 

The Company will continue to provide you with your current medical insurance coverage until [DATE]. Upon termination of your medical insurance coverage, you will have the right to elect continuation of your coverage at your expense in accordance with COBRA; and 

Any inquiries regarding Your employment will be responded to in a limited manner so as to release only the dates of Your employment with the Company and any other information that you specifically authorize (in writing) the Company to release. You should direct all reference checks only to Kirby Winters.



2.	Release:  Employee hereby irrevocably and unconditionally releases, acquits, and forever discharges the Company and/or its parent corporation, subsidiaries, divisions, predecessors, successors, and assigns, as well as each’s past and present officers, directors, employees, shareholders, trustees, joint venturers, partners, and anyone claiming through them (hereinafter “Releasees” collectively), in each’s individual and/or corporate capacities, from any and all claims, liabilities, promises, actions, damages, attorney’s fees, and the like, known or unknown, which you ever had against any of the Releasees arising out of or relating to your employment with the Company and/or the termination of your employment with the Company.  Said claims include (to the extent applicable to Employer and recitation herein does not constitute any admission by Employer that said claims are applicable to Employer), but are not limited to: (1) employment discrimination (including claims of sex discrimination and/or sexual harassment) and retaliation under Title VII, age discrimination under the Age Discrimination in Employment Act, and/or any other relevant federal or state statutes or municipal ordinances; (2) disputed wages; (3) wrongful discharge and/or breach of any alleged employment contract; (4) claims based on any tort, such as invasion of privacy, defamation, fraud, and infliction of emotional distress; (5) Fair Labor Standards Act; (6) the Occupational Safety and Health Act; (7) the Family Medical Leave Act; and (7) any other federal, state, or local civil or human rights law or any other local, state, or federal law, regulation, or ordinance, or any public policy, contract, tort or common law.  



Employee acknowledges and agrees that she shall not be eligible for, or entitled to receive and therefore releases and waives any claim for, compensation from Company (including but not limited to base pay, salary, commissions, bonuses, stock or stock options, employment benefits (including medical or insurance benefits), unemployment benefits or vacation pay except as set forth in paragraph 1 of this Agreement and all of its subparagraphs.



Employee covenants that she will not file, nor will she voluntarily participate or assist in the prosecution of any legal proceedings against Employer or any related entities, provided that nothing in this paragraph shall prevent Employee's participation in any such proceeding in compliance with a summons that requires such participation, or Employee's filing of a charge or otherwise participating in administrative proceedings or investigations of the EEOC, and nothing in this Section shall apply to challenges to ADEA waivers.



3.	Knowing and Voluntary Agreement. Each party hereto represents, declares, and agrees that she or it voluntarily accepts the provisions of this Agreement for the purposes of making a full and final compromise, adjustment and settlement of all claims herein described. Employee is advised to consult an attorney. Employee understands the effect of signing this Agreement.



4.	Review Period.  Employee acknowledges that she has been given at least twenty-one (21) days within which to consider this Agreement before its execution. This Agreement shall not become effective until seven (7) calendar days after the date of execution by Employee (the “Effective Date”). During this seven-day period, Employee may revoke this Agreement by notifying Employer in writing. Upon expiration of the seven-day period, Employee acknowledges that this Agreement becomes final and binding.



5.	No Disparagement. Employee agrees that she will not disparage Employer in any manner harmful to the Employer's business or business reputation. Employee further agrees that as of the effective date of this agreement, as defined above, Employee shall not represent herself or hold herself out as a current employee, consultant, or officer of Employer, or as holding any other current position with Employer. 



6.	Entire Agreement. This Agreement, when executed, contains the entire agreement between the parties and there are no other understandings or agreements, written or oral, between them on the subject except as expressly stated herein. This Agreement fully supersedes and replaces any and all prior agreements or understandings, if any, between Employee and Employer on any matter that is addressed in this Agreement. This Agreement cannot be amended or modified except by a written document signed by both Employer and Employee.



7.	Assignment by Employee. The rights and benefits of Employee under this Separation Agreement are personal to her and no such right or benefit shall be subject to voluntary or involuntary alienation, assignment, or transfer.



8.	Consequences of Violation of This Agreement.  If either party hereto violates any of the promises contained in this Agreement, then that party shall pay for all costs incurred by any of the released parties, including reasonable attorneys' fees, in defending against that party's claims and shall be liable for all expenses (including attorneys' fees) that are incurred in defending the suit.



9.		Confidentiality.  Employee will continue to comply with the terms of the Proprietary and Confidentiality Agreement between Employee and the Company and know and understand that the obligations contained in that agreement survive execution of this Agreement and Employee’s separation of employment.  In particular, Employee shall not disclose any confidential or proprietary information (specifically including pricing, margins, key customer contacts, and their profiles not generally known to the public) which Employee acquired as an employee of the Company to any other person or entity, or use such information in any manner that is detrimental to the interest of the Company.  A copy of Your Confidentiality Agreement is attached as Exhibit 1.



Both parties agree to keep the existence of, the terms of, and the details of this Agreement confidential, except that the parties may disclose information about this Agreement to their attorney, accountant, or financial advisors, as well as any court or government agency pursuant to an official request by such government agency, court order, or legally enforceable subpoena.  



10.	No Admission of Wrongdoing.  This Agreement is not and should not be construed as an admission of wrongdoing on the part of either party.  



11.	Severability.  The invalidity, illegality, or unenforceability of a provision or provisions of this Agreement will not affect any other provision of this Agreement, which will remain in full force and effect, nor will the invalidity, illegality, or unenforceability of a portion of any provision of this Agreement affect the balance of such provision. In the event that any one or more of the provisions contained in this Agreement or any portion thereof shall for any reason be held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable in any respect, this Agreement shall be reformed, construed, and enforced as if such invalid, illegal, or unenforceable provision had never been contained herein.

12.	Future Employment.  You agree that the Company has no obligation to consider You for employment should You apply in the future.  



13.	Return of Company Property.  You agree to return all Company property in your possession within two (2) days of the Effective Date.

14.	Section 409A of the Code.  To the extent applicable, it is intended that the arrangements under this Agreement be in full compliance with Section 409A of the Internal Revenue Code, as amended (“Code”).  To the extent any provision in this Agreement is or will be in violation of Section 409A of the Code, the Agreement shall be amended in such a manner as the parties may agree such that the Agreement is or remains in compliance with Section 409A of the Code and the intent of the parties is maintained to the maximum extent possible.  Reference to Section 409A of the Code includes any proposed, temporary or final regulations, or any other guidance, promulgated with respect to such Section by the U.S. Department of the Treasury or the Internal Revenue Service.  

15.	Governing Law.  The laws of the State of Georgia shall govern this Agreement.  

16.	Employee Understands Agreement.  You represent that you fully understand your right to review all aspects of this Agreement with an attorney of your choice, that you have had the opportunity to consult with an attorney of your choice, that you have carefully read and fully understand all the provisions of this Agreement, and that you are freely, knowingly and voluntarily entering into this Agreement.



If you are willing to enter into this Agreement, please signify your acceptance in the space indicated below.  



Sincerely,



[YOURNAME]



Accepted and agreed to on 





Employee signature:      	

Date:      

Witness:      

Date:
